HomeAI PlannerBookingsProfile

Camping Guide

Sleep under the stars in the Himalayas. Curated campsites with safety tips, gear lists, and everything you need to know.

Easy

Rishikhola Riverside Camp

Rishikhola • 600m

Camp right by the confluence of Rishi and Rangeet rivers. The sound of flowing water, starlit skies, and riverside bonfires make this a soul-stirring experience.

Oct – Mar
Moderate

Kolakham Forest Camp

Kolakham • 1,800m

Camp in an oak-rhododendron forest with uninterrupted views of Kanchenjunga. Wake up to birdsong and the golden glow of the world's third-highest peak.

Mar – May, Oct – Nov
Hard

Sandakphu Summit Camp

Sandakphu • 3,636m

The ultimate high-altitude camping experience. Sleep at 3,636m under a blanket of stars with four of the five highest peaks visible at sunrise.

Apr – May, Oct – NovPermit Required
Easy

Chatakpur Eco-Camp

Chatakpur • 2,400m

A community-run eco-camp inside a rhododendron sanctuary. Zero-waste philosophy with compost toilets and solar everything.

Mar – May, Sep – Nov
Easy

Mirik Pine Camp

Mirik • 1,495m

Relaxed lakeside camping under dense pine cover. Perfect for families and first-time campers who want comfort with nature.

Year-round
Easy

Sittong Orange Grove Camp

Sittong • 1,400m

Camp amidst orange orchards where the air smells of citrus. A unique low-altitude camping experience with village hospitality.

Dec – Jan (orange season)

Essential Camping Gear

Shelter

  • 4-season tent (for high altitude)
  • Groundsheet
  • Tent pegs & guy lines
  • Footprint

Sleep System

  • Sleeping bag (rated -5°C to 0°C)
  • Insulated sleeping pad
  • Inflatable pillow
  • Thermal liner

Cooking

  • Portable stove + fuel
  • Lightweight cookware
  • Spork & mug
  • Water filter/purifier

Safety & Misc

  • First-aid kit
  • Headlamp + batteries
  • Multi-tool
  • Whistle + mirror

Himalayan Camping Safety Rules

Always inform someone of your camping location and expected return.
Never camp alone above 3,000m without a guide.
Store food in sealed containers — away from your tent.
Check weather forecasts 24 hours before departure.
Carry extra layers — mountain weather changes in minutes.
Leave no trace. Pack out everything you pack in.
Respect local customs — ask before camping near villages.
If you feel altitude sickness symptoms, descend immediately.
Emergency Helpline: Dial 108 (Ambulance) or 1070 (Disaster Management) — available 24/7 across the region.
